task: T39 — Wire HavocBot AI into the live loop (--host/--bots N currently spawn inert standing bots; BotController/BotBrain are complete, unit-tested, with ZERO live callers)
baseBehaviors: CALL-SITE: qcsrc/server/main.qc:372 — bot_serverframe() runs once per server frame inside StartFrame, AFTER the warmup-limit check (main.qc:367) and BEFORE anticheat_startframe/MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(SV_StartFrame) (373-374) and the FOREACH_CLIENT PlayerPostThink/PlayerFrame loop (377-383). NOTE: the prompt's 'bot_navigation_ready' symbol does NOT exist in this Base checkout — all gating lives inside bot_serverframe itself.

bot_serverframe (bot/default/bot.qc:689-833): (a) intermission: bots stay unless ALL real clients left, then all dropclient'd (691-702); (b) game_stopped → return (704); (c) time<2.5 → currentbots=-1 sentinel + return (712-716); currentbots==-1 → recount IS_BOT_CLIENTs (718-723); (d) skill autocvar resync each frame — crossing bot_ai_bunnyhop_skilloffset triggers waypoint_updatecost_foralllinks (725-736); (e) bot_calculate_stepheightvec() every frame: stepheightvec=sv_stepheight*z, jumpheight_vec=jumpvel²/(2g), jumpstepheightvec=step+0.85*jump (615-621); (f) bot_navigation_movemode = bot_navigation_ignoreplayers ? MOVE_NOMONSTERS : MOVE_NORMAL (739); (g) autoskill every 5s when skill_auto≠0 — ±1 skill on a 2-frag gap (741-747, 569-613); (h) bot_fixcount(false) when time>botframe_nextthink; spawn failure → nextthink=time+10 (749-753); (i) WAYPOINT LOAD-ONCE: first frame with (currentbots>0 || wp-editor): botframe_spawnedwaypoints=true → waypoint_loadall() + waypoint_load_links(); cached links present → also waypoint_load_hardwiredlinks (761-784); (j) STRATEGY TOKEN: cycle bot_strategytoken to the next bot WITHOUT a goalcurrent (skipping dead/frozen) — exactly one bot per frame may run goal-rating (786-813); (k) danger update: every bot_ai_dangerdetectioninterval (0.25) call botframe_updatedangerousobjects(bot_ai_dangerdetectionupdates=64) (815-822); (l) bot_custom_weapon_priority_setup() when currentbots>0 (831-832).

bot_fixcount (bot.qc:623-687): activerealplayers = real clients that are players; realplayers = all real clients. Target bots = bot_vs_human branch (643), else if (realplayers || bot_join_empty || (currentbots>0 && time<5)): bots = max(bot_number, minplayers - activerealplayers) [teamplay: minplayers_per_team*AVAILABLE_TEAMS], capped by GetPlayerLimit() and maxclients-realplayers (644-660); else bots=0 (664). Adds ONE bot per frame (multiple_per_frame=false from serverframe, 667-681); while currentbots > target → bot_removenewest() (682-683) which in teamplay removes from the LARGEST team (533-539, 486-531). KEY INTERPLAY: a host that adds bots without raising bot_number will have fixcount REMOVE them at time 2.5+.

bot_spawn (bot.qc:45-60): spawnclient → ++currentbots → bot_setnameandstuff → ClientConnect → bot_setclientfields (accuracy-share cvars) → PutClientInServer; INFO_JOIN_PLAY notification when !teamplay. bot_clientconnect (469-484): bot_nextthink = time - random() (staggers first thinks), isbot=true, havocbot_setupbot (havocbot.qc:1763-1771 sets .bot_ai=havocbot_ai + havocbot_chooserole).

bot_setnameandstuff (bot.qc:163-349): reads bot_config_file (=bots.txt, xonotic-server.cfg:123) — tab-separated rows: name, model, skin, shirt, pants, forced-team, then 12 skill modifiers each ±random when blank (keyboard*0.5, move*2, dodge*2, ping*0.5, weapon*2, aggression*1, rangepreference*1, aim*2, offset*2, mouse*1, think*1, ai*2; bot.qc:275-290); weighted random row (prio+1 unused name, +2 smallest team); name fallback 'Bot', duplicates get '(N)', wrapped in bot_prefix('[BOT]')/bot_suffix(''); model gets '.iqm' appended, playermodel='models/player/'+model; setcolor(shirt*16+pants).

bot_think (bot.qc:62-161) IS CALLED FROM THE PER-CLIENT PHYSICS STEP, NOT StartFrame: ecs/systems/sv_physics.qc:41-46 sys_phys_ai(this){ if IS_BOT_CLIENT → bot_think } invoked at ecs/systems/physics.qc:28, inside sys_phys_update AFTER sys_phys_monitor and BEFORE the movement simulation — the bot writes CS(this).movement / .v_angle / PHYS_INPUT_BUTTON_* and the SAME tick's player physics consumes them exactly like a human usercmd. Think throttle: bot_nextthink += max(0.01, bot_ai_thinkinterval(0.05) * min(14/(skill+bot_aiskill+14),1)); SUPERBOT(skill>100) 0.005 (71-75). bot_god → FL_GODMODE (67-69). Fake ping = bound(0, 0.07-bound(0,(skill+pingskill)*0.005,0.05)+random()*0.01, 0.65) (104). Clears ALL buttons each think; JUMP stays held while time < bot_jump_time+0.2 (ramp jumps, 110-120). Pre-game countdown → movement='0 0 0' (122-127). DEAD: aistatus=0, movement=0; DEAD_DYING → jump=false, DEAD_DEAD → jump=TRUE (presses jump to respawn, 129-149). AI_STATUS_STUCK → navigation_unstuck (150-151). warmup → auto-ready (153-157). Then this.bot_ai(this) = havocbot_ai (160).

havocbot_ai (havocbot/havocbot.qc:35-213): bot_aimdir_executed latch (40-43, locked after teleport); bot scripting + pause early-outs (45-50); STRATEGY-TOKEN HOLDER ONLY runs this.havocbot_role (goal rating/routing) — skipped while on jumppad/frozen/coming off a JUMP waypoint (52-104); stale/freed goal → clearroute (106-111); dead/frozen → clearroute+enemy=NULL (113-119); havocbot_chooseenemy (121); per-weapon-slot havocbot_chooseweapon every bot_ai_chooseweaponinterval=0.5 (123-132); havocbot_aim (133); WITH ENEMY: aistatus=ATTACKING and PER-WEAPON w.wr_aim(w,this,weaponentity) — each weapon's QC method calls bot_aim(actor,wepent,shotspeed,upspeed,lifetime,applygravity,accurate) and sets ATCK/ATCK2 itself (135-160; e.g. vortex.qc wr_aim: bot_aim(...,1000000,0,1,false,true); devastator.qc:339+ boosts speed by sqrt(guiderate)*20/9.489 and skill>=2 simulates rocket detonation via ATCK2); bot_nofire/independent suppresses fire (142-146); havocbot_movetogoal (173); fallback bot_aimdir toward goal with z flattened (174-181); idle reload at skill>=2/>=5 (183-212).

havocbot_movetogoal (havocbot.qc:446-1332) — the steering core: crouch from WAYPOINTFLAG_CROUCH (459); jetpack navigation (463-524); JUMPPAD handling incl. AI_STATUS_OUT_JUMPPAD stuck-recovery to the farthest visible waypoint (527-614); TRIGGER_HURT-BELOW evasion at skill>6 while airborne: jetpack / devastator rocketjump (v_angle_x=90, ATCK, ATCK2 after detonatedelay) / plain forward (616-721); underwater no-goal swim-up (723-736); goal maintenance: respawning-item wait (bot_pickup_respawning, 755-772), navigation_shortenpath, poptouchedgoals, dead-player-goal → nearby loot (793-836); OBSTACLE JUMP: tracebox at foot → +stepheightvec → +jumpheight, jump if raised trace goes farther (1047-1100); havocbot_checkgoaldistance no-progress detector — 0.5s not getting closer → tracewalk re-verify, 2nd failure → clearroute (344-368, 1102-1128); DANGER AHEAD: dst_ahead = origin+view_ofs+(velocity*0.2 or flatdir*32) → havocbot_checkdanger → AI_STATUS_DANGER_AHEAD, evadedanger lateral vector from the prev-wp→goal line scaled bound(1,3-(skill+dodgeskill),3), do_break backward (1136-1182, 1249-1270); player-goal + danger = unreachable → ignoregoal for bot_ai_ignoregoal_timeout=3 (1176-1182); havocbot_dodge projectile dodging — NOW SUPERBOT-ONLY (1773-1779: 'if (!SUPERBOT) return 0 0 0'), dodge scaled bound(0,0.5+(skill+dodgeskill)*0.1,1), dodge_enemy_factor=bound(0,(skill+dodgeskill)/7,1) shrinks the move when enemy has LOS (1185-1204); ladder z-steering (1207-1228); FINAL: makevectors(v_angle); CS movement = dir·v_forward/right/up * maxspeed (1272-1278); SUPERBOT random combat strafe (1281-1307); keyboard emulation at skill<10 (havocbot_keyboard_movement 272-341: quantizes movement to keys past bot_ai_keyboard_threshold=0.57, blends by dist/bot_ai_keyboard_distance=250); bunnyhop when skill+moveskill >= bot_ai_bunnyhop_skilloffset=7 (215-270, 1314-1316); dir·v_up >= jumpvel*0.5 && onground → JUMP (1318-1319); dodge-driven random jump/crouch (1320-1331).

havocbot_checkdanger (havocbot.qc:401-444): traceline eye→dst_ahead; if clear AND not on jumppad/hardwired-link/JUMP-prev AND (onground || ROAMING/RUNNING || jump pressed): trace dst_ahead 3000 down; if floor below feet level: SKY surface → 1, floor >100 below min(feet, goal.z) → 2, pointcontents(endpos+1z) LAVA/SLIME → 3, tracebox_hits_trigger_hurt → 4 (line check then tracebox confirm). Caller maps 1-3 → danger (brake/evade), 4 → unreachable if goal is above jump height else danger (1141-1153).

havocbot_chooseenemy (havocbot.qc:1334-1471): bot_nofire → enemy=NULL; keep current while bot_shouldattack + sticking window (havocbot_stickenemy_time = bot_ai_enemydetectioninterval_stickingtoenemy=4; re-check: LOS + within 1000 → extend 0.5s, 1342-1366); re-scan every bot_ai_enemydetectioninterval=2 (SUPERBOT 0.1, 1367-1373); scan g_bot_targets with .bot_attack within bot_ai_enemydetectionradius=10000: nearest with eye-traceline LOS (SUPERBOT rates bound(50,hp+armor,250)*distance instead, 1389-1440); misc_breakablemodel only as secondary pass; weaponless bots re-scan through DPCONTENTS_OPAQUE (transparent walls) (1442-1462).

havocbot_chooseweapon (havocbot.qc:1495-1607): no enemy → first owned weapon from the MID priority list; combo gate: same weapon refired within ATTACK_FINISHED > time + bot_ai_weapon_combo_threshold(0.4)*(4-0.3*(skill+weaponskill)) → keep for 1s (1538-1559); else pick first client_hasweapon from FAR/MID/CLOSE lists by distance buckets — distance=bound(10, dist-200, 10000)*2^rangepreference vs bot_ai_custom_weapon_priority_distances '300 850'; lists parsed once in bot_custom_weapon_priority_setup (bot.qc:351-410) from xonotic-server.cfg:155-157 (far: vaporizer oknex vortex rifle electro devastator mortar...; close: vaporizer oknex vortex okshotgun shotgun ... machinegun arc ...).

aim.qc bot_aimdir/bot_aim: fake-latency aiming via 5 chained low-pass filters (order_mix_1st..5th = 0.01/0.075/0.01/0.0375/0.01, order_filter = 0.2/0.2/0.1/0.2/0.25, xonotic-server.cfg:172-181), bot_badaimoffset error scaled by bot_ai_aimskill_offset=1.8 (cfg:152), turn rate from aimskill_fixedrate=15/blendrate=2 (cfg:146-147), mouse jitter aimskill_mouse=1/think=1; fire gate = deviation cone + aggression random; firetolerance=1 (cfg:148). [Already faithfully ported in BotAim.cs.]

navigation_unstuck (navigation.qc:1908-2007): requires bot_wander_enable=1 + at least one non-GENERATED waypoint; ONE global queue owner (bot_waypoint_queue_owner) walks all waypoints within 1000qu, tracewalk-rates each, finally routes to the FARTHEST reachable one and clears AI_STATUS_STUCK; meanwhile randomly roams candidate waypoints. Triggered from bot_think:150-151 when aistatus & AI_STATUS_STUCK.

botframe_updatedangerousobjects (navigation.qc:1874-1906): for up to maxupdate(=bot_ai_dangerdetectionupdates=64) waypoints, it.dmg = Σ over g_bot_dodge items of max(0, linearcost(bot_dodgerating) - travelcost(item→nearest point of wp box)) with LOS traceline — a per-waypoint danger cost added into path rating.

WAYPOINT FILES (waypoints.qc): waypoint_loadall (1837+) opens maps/<mapname><gt_ext>.waypoints, falling back to maps/<mapname>.waypoints when gt_ext≠'' fails (1845-1850); link cache maps/*.waypoints.cache (1324-1329, same fallback), hardwired links maps/*.waypoints.hardwired (1472-1477). Loaded ONCE on the first bot_serverframe with bots present.

portState: DEAD CODE (complete, tested, zero live callers): src/XonoticGodot.Server/Bot/* — BotController.cs (185 lines: AddBot/RemoveBot/Frame/roster/SkillOf, SpawnBot host hook, DefaultSpawn fallback), BotBrain.cs (483: full havocbot_ai think — ChooseEnemy:399-442, ChooseWeapon:285-315 hitscan/splash distance buckets reading bot_ai_custom_weapon_priority_distances, AimAndDecideFire:206-231 w/ lead+ballistic arc+LOS, CombatMovement:243-272 strafe-clock dodge + health-advantage charge/retreat, ShouldAttack:451-482 faithful bot_shouldattack), BotAim.cs (278: faithful aim.qc filter chain, badaim offset, fire cone+aggression, SUPERBOT snap), BotNavigation.cs (335: goal stack, SetGoal A*, Steer = obstacle-jump tracebox cascade + edge-brake + bunnyhop:253-285), BotTracewalk.cs, BotRoles.cs/BotObjectiveRoles.cs (per-gametype roles), Waypoint.cs (765: WaypointNetwork + LoadFromText + LoadLinks cache + LoadHardwiredLinks + AutoLink + ForMap:580-600 one-call load-or-autogenerate, perf-fixed per memory). Only referenced by tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/BotNavTests.cs + BotPerfBench.cs.

CRITICAL SEAM — BotBrain.Think APPLIES ITS OWN MOVE: BotBrain.cs:198 calls Movement.Move(bot, input) at the end of Think, and writes bot.Angles itself (195). Meanwhile GameWorld.OnClientMove (GameWorld.cs:810-896) ALSO calls Movement.Move(p, input) at :886 for every sim client including bots — with ZeroInput, because ServerNet.ProvideInput (game/net/ServerNet.cs:625-628) returns ZeroInput for any Player not in _byPlayer (bots have no peer). Wiring Think as-is would double-integrate physics each tick.

--bots N FLOW (bots spawn but stand still): Main.cs:98-100 parses --bots → Shell.BootBots (game/Shell.cs:49-50) → MatchConfig.BotCount → NetGame (Shell.cs:116, 490-491). game/net/NetGame.cs:510-515: boot loop calls _serverWorld.Clients.ClientConnect(isBot:true, netName:"[BOT] <name>") + sets Player.BotSkill; names from a hardcoded 8-name table (NetGame.cs:2330-2334), NOT bots.txt. NetGame.cs:537-563 already wires Commands.AddBotHandler/RemoveBotHandler (live bot_add/bot_remove). No brain is ever constructed; no waypoints are ever loaded; memory's 'BotBrain.Think has no caller' confirmed.

HUMAN INPUT PATH (the pipeline bot input must ride): ServerNet ctor sets _world.InputProvider = ProvideInput (ServerNet.cs:183). GameWorld.OnClientMove:846 pulls InputProvider(p) once for movement; :865-869 applies input.ViewAngles to p.Angles/ViewAngles ONLY for !p.IsBot (comment: bots steer by writing their OWN Angles in BotBrain); :884-892 Movement.Move + AntiCheat (skipped for bots); OnPlayerPostThink:904-934 → WeaponThink:943-958 pulls InputProvider(p) AGAIN (ServerNet caches per tick via TickInputTime, ServerNet.cs:636-638) and runs WeaponFireDriver.Frame(p, input) — attack buttons fire weapons. Dead players: DeadPlayerThink (GameWorld.cs:1461-1526) advances DEAD_* off input buttons; RespawnTiming.cs:70-77 sets RespawnFlag.Force for ALL bots → bots already auto-respawn without input.

BOT CLIENT LIFECYCLE (already works): ClientManager.ClientConnect(isBot:true) (ClientManager.cs:141-213) registers entity table/sim Clients/score row/gametype roster/team, then bots AUTO-JOIN at connect (:206-210) → Spawn → SpawnSystem.PutPlayerInServer gives the default model (sv_defaultplayermodel → models/player/erebus.iqm, SpawnSystem.cs:100-104). ClientDisconnect (:545-569) full cleanup. ClientManager has NO bot-brain hook of any kind.

BOTS ALREADY NETWORK TO CLIENTS: ServerNet.BuildEntitySet (ServerNet.cs:834-897) snapshots every non-observer Player; NetIdFor (:1093) mints stable ids for non-peer players ('humans AND bots get a stable id', :853); model networked by NAME (:887); names reach clients via the scoreboard rows ScoreRowWire(NetIdFor(p), columns, p.NetName, team) (:1051-1052). NetGame.cs:526-529 comment confirms 'no extra net plumbing needed'. So question (6) is already satisfied once bots move.

WAYPOINT LOAD: WaypointNetwork.ForMap (Waypoint.cs:580-600) — .waypoints + .waypoints.cache (skips the O(N²) AutoLink) + .waypoints.hardwired, else GenerateFromEntities; vtos single-quote parse fixed (memory: bot-nav perf pass). VFS paths proven in tests: vfs.ReadText($"maps/{Map}.waypoints") (BotPerfBench.cs:82-83). NO live caller. GameWorld has the needed reader already: ConfigReader (GameWorld.cs:196, set by NetGame.cs:425-426 to the VFS) + MapName (:157) + Pvs (:355-356, set by NetGame.cs:423 — CheckPvs for bot LOS is a conservative no-op when absent).

CONSOLE COMMANDS EXIST: src/XonoticGodot.Server/Commands.cs:260-264 registers bot_add/bot_remove/setbots/removebots; :112-115 AddBotHandler/RemoveBotHandler host sinks; :582-601 CmdSetBots sets bot_number on SettempCvars then add/removes via handlers to converge immediately. NO Commands.cs change required for T39.

CVAR FALLBACKS DIVERGE FROM SHIPPED CFG (only matters when assets aren't mounted — GameWorld.Boot:332-337 loads the real cfg tree over them via ConfigLoader when ConfigReader is wired): src/XonoticGodot.Server/Cvars.cs:213-244 ships skill=5 (cfg: 8), bot_typefrag=1 (cfg: 0), bot_ai_strategyinterval=5 (7), enemydetectioninterval=0.5 (2), _stickingtoenemy=0.2 (4), aimskill_offset=0.3 (1.8), timeitems_minrespawndelay=11 (25). minplayers/minplayers_per_team seeded at Cvars.cs:88-89.

MISSING vs Base (the port brain has no equivalent): bot_serverframe population loop (bot_fixcount autofill from bot_number/minplayers/bot_vs_human/bot_join_empty + one-per-frame add + remove-newest/largest-team); strategy-token rotation (BotBrain.cs:143-147 uses random jitter on a 2s-skill-scaled strategy clock instead — documented divergence); navigation_unstuck + AI_STATUS_STUCK; havocbot_checkgoaldistance no-progress detector; havocbot_checkdanger (lava/slime/trigger_hurt/sky/edge — BotNavigation.cs:208-220 has only a height-based edge brake; no PointContents, no trigger_hurt test); botframe_updatedangerousobjects (Waypoint has no per-node danger cost); per-weapon wr_aim specials (devastator guided-speed + skill>=2 detonation/ATCK2, electro combos — port uses one generic g_balance_<wep>_primary_speed read, BotBrain.cs:364-373); keyboard emulation (skill<10); fake ping; bot_god; jetpack/rocketjump trigger_hurt escape; jumppad OUT_JUMPPAD recovery; bots.txt name/model/skill table (hardcoded 8 names in NetGame); autoskill; skill-scaled think throttle (port thinks every tick); live `skill` cvar resync onto existing brains; bot_ai_thinkinterval.

Engine support present for the gaps: ITraceService.PointContents (src/XonoticGodot.Common/Services/Services.cs:41, impl TraceService.cs:113); trigger_hurt entities spawn with ClassName=='trigger_hurt' (MapObjectsRegistry.cs:40, Triggers.cs:187-203) and are findable via Api.Entities.FindByClass; SuperContents lava/slime masks exist (BotTracewalk.cs:184).
plan: 1. BotBrain: add a produce-only think path. Split Think (BotBrain.cs:121-199) so the input-assembly half can run WITHOUT the trailing Movement.Move — e.g. `public MovementInput ThinkProduce(Player bot, float dt)` that fills LastInput and returns it, with the existing `Think` kept as ThinkProduce+Movement.Move for BotPerfBench/standalone compat. Keep the brain's own bot.Angles write (GameWorld:863-869 deliberately doesn't clobber bot angles).

2. New file src/XonoticGodot.Server/Bot/BotPopulation.cs — port bot_serverframe + bot_fixcount semantics over ClientManager: per-frame ServerFrame(world): (a) intermission rule (drop all bots only when HumanCount==0); (b) game-stopped early-out; (c) Time<2.5 sentinel + recount; (d) target = bot_vs_human branch / max(bot_number, minplayers fill) capped by maxclients, else 0 when no humans && !bot_join_empty (grace: currentbots>0 && Time<5); (e) ONE add per frame via ClientManager.ClientConnect(isBot:true) + brain creation; remove-newest (teamplay: from largest team) while above target; (f) live `skill` cvar resync onto every brain each frame (QC bot.qc:725-736); (g) danger-update scheduler stub on bot_ai_dangerdetectioninterval (call into the new waypoint danger pass when implemented); (h) optional faithful strategy-token rotation: hand exactly one brain per frame the 'may re-rate goals' token (replacing/augmenting the jitter clock in BotBrain.cs:143-147).

3. GameWorld (OWNED): add `public BotPopulation Bots` + a brains map (Player→BotBrain). (a) Boot: after SpawnMapEntities, load waypoints ONCE via ConfigReader — try maps/<MapName><gt_ext>.waypoints then maps/<MapName>.waypoints (+.cache/.hardwired, QC waypoints.qc:1845-1850) → WaypointNetwork.ForMap(...); fallback GenerateFromEntities; hand to Bots. (b) OnStartFrame (GameWorld.cs:736-782): call Bots.ServerFrame(...) after AntiCheat.StartFrame (:762) and before ServerHooks.FireStartFrame (:778) — matching main.qc:372's position. (c) OnClientMove (:810-896): resolve input as `IMovementInput input = p.IsBot ? Bots.InputFor(p, Simulation.FrameTime) : InputProvider(p);` where InputFor runs brain.ThinkProduce once per tick (cache keyed on Time, like ServerNet.cs:636-638) — the brain runs INSIDE the bot's client step right before its movement, faithful to sys_phys_ai (ecs/physics.qc:28). Movement.Move(:886), DeadPlayerThink(:852-856 — bot now presses jump like QC bot.qc:144-147, with RespawnFlag.Force kept as safety) and WeaponThink all consume the SAME command. (d) WeaponThink (:943-958): same p.IsBot branch so ButtonAttack1/2 reach WeaponFireDriver.Frame — bots fire through exactly the human path (lag-comp simply doesn't rewind for a bot shooter: no peer state, acceptable).

4. ClientManager (OWNED): add `public Action<Player>? OnBotConnected` / reuse OnClientConnect+OnClientDisconnect — GameWorld wires these to create the BotBrain (skill from Player.BotSkill, role from GameType.NetName, PlayerProvider = () => Clients.Players, Network = the map graph) on connect and drop it on disconnect. Brain Aim/Nav hull+view synced from the spawned player (it already does this in the ctor).

5. Port the missing brain pieces (priority order): (a) havocbot_checkdanger into BotNavigation/new BotDanger.cs — eye→ahead traceline + 3000u down-trace; classify sky / >100-below-goal drop / PointContents lava|slime / trigger_hurt box overlap (FindInRadius or FindByClass('trigger_hurt') + AABB vs the down-trace column); feed a danger flag into Steer to brake/evade exactly like havocbot.qc:1136-1182; ALSO run it against the dodge vector (1201-1204). (b) havocbot_checkgoaldistance no-progress unstuck (0.5s not closer → tracewalk re-verify → clearroute+re-rate; second failure forces re-plan) — covers most of navigation_unstuck's value without the global queue. (c) button semantics: keep jump held 0.2s after WantJump (bot_jump_time, bot.qc:112) so ramp/bhop jumps register across ticks. (d) bot think throttle: bot_ai_thinkinterval * min(14/(skill+14),1) so low-skill bots are sluggish (QC bot.qc:71-75); repeat LastInput between thinks (like ServerNet's starve-repeat). (e) OPTIONAL follow-ups, flag as not-blocking: keyboard emulation, devastator wr_aim detonation, waypoint danger costs, navigation_unstuck queue, bots.txt table, autoskill, fake ping, bot_god.

6. NetGame.cs (NOT a chokepoint, light touch): replace the boot ClientConnect loop (NetGame.cs:510-515) with seeding the server store's bot_number (= _botCount) — the new fixcount fills one-per-frame exactly like QC and, critically, won't REMOVE host-added bots at the time-2.5 recount (QC bot.qc:682 removes while currentbots > target). Rewire AddBotHandler/RemoveBotHandler (:537-563) to GameWorld.Bots.AddBot/RemoveBot so direct adds also register brains (setbots already converges via bot_number, Commands.cs:582-593). Keep ForgetPlayer on removal.

7. Cvars.cs fallback alignment (non-chokepoint, defensive only since the real cfg overrides when mounted): skill 8, bot_typefrag 0, bot_ai_strategyinterval 7, enemydetectioninterval 2, _stickingtoenemy 4, aimskill_offset 1.8, timeitems_minrespawndelay 25 (xonotic-server.cfg:129/134/137/139/140/152/183).

8. Tests (new tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/BotLiveLoopTests.cs): fixcount math (bot_number fill 1/frame, minplayers-activehumans, removal above target, no-humans+!bot_join_empty teardown, time<2.5 sentinel); GameWorld integration: boot floor world + spawnpoints, set bot_number=2, Frame() 5 sim-seconds → 2 bots joined+spawned, origins moved, a planted enemy player gets fired at (WeaponFireDriver consumed ButtonAttack1 — assert ammo/shot fired), bot dies → auto-respawns; waypoint ForMap loads via a fake ConfigReader; danger test: lava pit ahead → bot brakes/never enters (PointContents stub). Keep BotPerfBench compiling (Think still applies movement).

9. Manual verify: dotnet build XonoticGodot.csproj; run `--host stormkeep --bots 4 --screenshot shots/bots.png` windowed — bots visible moving/shooting, scoreboard shows [BOT] names; console `setbots 2`, `bot_add`, `removebots`; `developer 1` for [nudge]/[death] diagnostics; confirm 72Hz tick budget holds (BotPerfBench numbers: amortized SetGoal ~ms-scale).
filesToCreate: src/XonoticGodot.Server/Bot/BotPopulation.cs (bot_serverframe + bot_fixcount port: fill/remove scheduler, skill resync, strategy token, danger-update cadence)
src/XonoticGodot.Server/Bot/BotDanger.cs (havocbot_checkdanger + tracebox_hits_trigger_hurt port; or fold into BotNavigation.cs)
filesToModify: src/XonoticGodot.Server/GameWorld.cs (OWNED: Bots property, Boot waypoint load via ConfigReader, OnStartFrame → Bots.ServerFrame, OnClientMove/WeaponThink bot-input branch)
src/XonoticGodot.Server/Bot/BotBrain.cs (produce-only ThinkProduce, jump-held window, think throttle, danger integration)
src/XonoticGodot.Server/Bot/BotController.cs (live-loop adjustments: per-tick input cache, ClientManager-routed spawn; or supersede with BotPopulation)
game/net/NetGame.cs (boot bot fill → bot_number seed; AddBot/RemoveBot handlers → GameWorld.Bots) — NOT in the chokepoint list, but flag to orchestrator: other wave tasks may touch it
src/XonoticGodot.Server/Cvars.cs (align bot_* fallback defaults with shipped xonotic-server.cfg)
chokepointSnippets: Commands.cs — NO CHANGE NEEDED. bot_add/bot_remove/setbots/removebots already exist (Commands.cs:260-264) and route through AddBotHandler/RemoveBotHandler (:112-115, :519, :527, :592-593, :601). One interplay to verify, not change: CmdSetBots writes bot_number via SettempCvars.Set("bot_number", n) (:588) — confirm SettempCvars is the SERVER world's store so the new BotPopulation fixcount reads the same value (if it wrote elsewhere, fixcount would fight setbots). If the owner of Commands.cs is making changes anyway, an optional 1-line improvement: make CmdSetBots NOT also loop the handlers (:592-593) once fixcount converges on bot_number itself — otherwise harmless (fixcount sees currentbots==target).

ServerNet.cs — NO CHANGE NEEDED. ProvideInput's bots-not-in-_byPlayer → ZeroInput behavior (ServerNet.cs:625-628) becomes dead-path for bots once GameWorld branches on p.IsBot before consulting InputProvider; the comment at GameWorld.cs:843-845 ('Bots have no input queue...') should be updated by the T39 owner (GameWorld is owned by T39). Bot snapshots/names/ids already work: NetIdFor (:1093) stable ids for non-peer players, ScoreRowWire carries p.NetName (:1051-1052), model by name (:887). Lag-comp: a bot SHOOTER gets no rewind (no PeerState ping) — faithful enough (QC gives bots fake ping); no ServerNet change requested.
ClientNet.cs / NetProtocol.cs — NO CHANGE NEEDED. Bots ride the existing Player entity kind + scoreboard wire format; no new fields, no protocol bump.
DamageSystem.cs — NO CHANGE NEEDED. Bot damage/obituaries flow through the existing player path (bots are Players with score rows).
MapObjectsRegistry.cs / Registries.cs — NO CHANGE NEEDED. trigger_hurt is already registered (MapObjectsRegistry.cs:40) and discoverable by classname for the new BotDanger checks.
risks: DOUBLE-MOVE: BotBrain.Think currently calls Movement.Move (BotBrain.cs:198) AND GameWorld.OnClientMove:886 moves every sim client — wiring Think unmodified double-integrates physics. The produce-only split is mandatory; keep the old Think applying movement so BotPerfBench/BotNavTests still compile+pass.

FIXCOUNT REMOVAL TRAP: porting bot_fixcount faithfully means bots above the bot_number/minplayers target get REMOVED (bot.qc:682-683). NetGame's current boot loop adds bots WITHOUT raising bot_number → the new fixcount would tear them down at Time≥2.5. The NetGame boot must seed bot_number (or BotPopulation must take a host floor target).

TICK BUDGET: brains now run inside the 72Hz loop. BotPerfBench measured SetGoal as the hot path (amortized ms-scale after the nav perf pass); the QC-faithful mitigations are the strategy token (1 goal-search/frame across all bots) + the skill-scaled think throttle — implement at least one before shipping >4 bots. Danger update capped at 64 waypoints/0.25s like QC.

INPUT CACHE CORRECTNESS: GameWorld pulls input twice per tick (movement :846 + WeaponThink :956). The bot branch must cache the produced input per sim tick (key on Simulation/world Time like ServerNet.cs:636-638) or the brain thinks twice and fire-edge state desyncs.

OBSERVER/JOIN EDGES: bots auto-Join at connect (ClientManager.cs:206-210); if Spawn fails (no spawnpoint yet) the bot falls back to observer — BotPopulation must not count perpetually-observing bots as filled, and the brain must early-out for IsObserver (QC bot_think:130-148 movement=0).

PITCH SIGN: QMath VecToAngles/AngleVectors pitch is intentionally DP-inverse (memory note) — BotAim already compensates (BotAim.cs:150 ang.X*=-1); any new aim code must use the same convention or bots aim into the floor.

WAYPOINT FILE ABSENCE: many stock maps ship no .waypoints; GenerateFromEntities fallback gives item/spawn-based graphs only — bots will roam but may miss jumppad/teleport routes. Don't gate bot movement on graph presence (QC bots still chase visible enemies/items with no waypoints).

DIVERGENCE BOOKKEEPING: jitter-clock instead of strategy token, generic shot-speed instead of per-weapon wr_aim, no keyboard emulation/fake ping — all change bot 'feel' vs stock at a given skill. Document in the port-header notes; the skill 8 shipped default makes bots noticeably competent, so under-aggressive bots will read as a bug.

TEST GLOBAL STATE: bot tests boot GameInit/Api.Services — suite already runs single-threaded (TestParallelization.cs memory note); follow the existing Boot-per-test pattern in BotPerfBench to avoid cross-test bleed.
testPlan: Unit — BotPopulation/fixcount: target math for bot_number=3/minplayers=4 with 1 active human → 3; one add per Frame (3 frames to fill); removal above target removes newest (teamplay: largest team); humans all leave + bot_join_empty=0 → bots removed only after intermission rule; Time<2.5 sentinel no-op; live skill cvar change propagates to brains next frame.
Unit — BotDanger: synthetic lava brush ahead → checkdanger==3 path brakes; trigger_hurt entity in the down-column → 4 + unreachable when goal above jump height; clear floor → 0.

Integration — live loop: GameWorld over a flat-floor CollisionWorld with 2 info_player_deathmatch dicts; Cvars Set bot_number=2; world.Frame(5s of ticks): assert 2 IsBot players joined+spawned with default model, both origins moved >64u from spawn, no exceptions at 72Hz; plant a stationary human-flagged target with LOS → a bot acquires Enemy and WeaponFireDriver consumes ButtonAttack1 (ammo decremented / projectile entity exists); kill a bot → DeadPlayerThink + Force respawns it within RespawnTimeMax+1.

Integration — waypoints: fake ConfigReader serving maps/testmap.waypoints (+.cache) → GameWorld.Boot loads a WaypointNetwork with N nodes and zero AutoLink calls (cache path); absent files → GenerateFromEntities graph non-empty.
Regression: dotnet test tests/XonoticGodot.Tests (full suite, currently ~1059+; BotNavTests/BotPerfBench must still pass with the produce-only refactor).

Manual smoke: dotnet build XonoticGodot.csproj; run --host <map> --bots 4 windowed + --screenshot — bots visibly move/jump/shoot, scoreboard lists [BOT] names with scores changing; console: setbots 1 (peels to 1 within frames), bot_add Hellfire 9, removebots; reconnect a second client (--connect) and confirm bots render/animate remotely (networked by name).

Perf check: rerun BotPerfBench probe 5 (4 bots × 20 sim-seconds end-to-end Think) and compare allocations/ms before/after the live wiring — no per-tick allocation regressions in the produced-input path.
